perturbation.original_text: 8. Return of Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after any written request by Sanmina, the Recipient shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information except copies required for backup, disaster recovery, or business continuity and in such case the obligations hereunder shall survive until such copies are destroyed .
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Return of Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after any written request by Sanmina, the Recipient shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the phrase 'except copies required for backup, disaster recovery, or business continuity and in such case the obligations hereunder shall survive until such copies are destroyed' creates a contradiction. Without this exception, the clause mandates the return of *all* Confidential Information, even data necessary for critical business functions like disaster recovery. However, this requirement might be practically impossible to fulfill in real-world scenarios, directly contradicting the implicit understanding that a business needs to maintain necessary backups.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Duty to Protect. Recipient shall protect the disclosed Confidential Informat ion by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information as the Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient shall (i) 2 NDA Rev. 3 -14-14 Form NDA -3 (Supplier) limit access to all Confidential Information to its employees who shall reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above, (ii) shall insure that all such employees have signed a nondisc losure agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement, (iii) shall use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the Purpose, and (iv) shall use due diligence to insure that all such persons comply with the provisions of this Agr eement. Recipient shall be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its employees or representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for exceptions to the return of confidential information, such as for backup or disaster recovery. The changed text removes this exception, requiring the recipient to return *all* copies. This directly contradicts section 5, which states that the recipient must protect the confidential information with a reasonable degree of care, implicitly requiring them to maintain backups to ensure the safety and availability of the data. Removing the backup exception makes it impossible to comply with both clauses, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Court Orders. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall restrict Recipient from disclosing Confidential Information that is required to be disclosed under any subpoena or court order provided that the Recipient provides Sanmina with prompt notice so that Sanmina may, at its expense, seek a protective order or take other appropriate measures.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Court Orders. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall restrict Recipient from disclosing Confidential Information that is required to be disclosed under any subpoena or court order.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'provided that the Recipient provides Sanmina with prompt notice so that Sanmina may, at its expense, seek a protective order or take other appropriate measures', the Recipient is now *not* required to notify Sanmina before disclosing confidential information under a court order. This contradicts the overall spirit and intent of the NDA which is to protect Sanmina's Confidential Information, removing Sanmina's chance to protect it in the event of a court ordered disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations here under are necessary and reasonable in order to protect Sanmina and Sanmina’s business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages will be inadequate to compensate Sanmina for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, Recipient agrees and acknowledges that any such viola tion or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to Sanmina and that, in addition to any other remedies th at may be avail able, in law, in equity or otherwise, Sanmina will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages and without the necessity of posting bond.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 6 required the Recipient to notify Sanmina before disclosing confidential information under a court order, allowing Sanmina to seek a protective order. The changed text removes this notification requirement. This directly contradicts section 10, which emphasizes the importance of protecting Sanmina's business and confidential information and the inadequacy of monetary damages for any breach, as Sanmina is now unable to prevent the breach.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their agreement. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be o f any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their agreement. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be o f any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.', it opens the door to potentially allowing oral modifications of the agreement. This directly contradicts the statement that the document is the 'complete and exclusive Agreement' because it implies that changes may not need to be in writing. If there is a disagreement as to weather oral agreements have been made, it would be difficult for this document to be considered complete and exclusive as the document states.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their agreement. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the sentence 'No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be o f any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto' from section 12 directly contradicts the statement that 'This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties'. Without the removed sentence, oral modifications become a possibility, thus the document can no longer be considered complete and exclusive, creating a contradiction within the document itself as the contradicted text is also section 12.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law princip les. The parties agree that the state courts of Santa Clara County, California and the federal courts located in the Northern District of the State of California shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. The parties consent to the exercise by such courts of personal jurisdiction over them and each party waives any objection it might otherwise have to venue, personal jurisdiction, inconvenience of forum, and any similar or related doctrine.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law princip les. The parties agree that the state courts of Santa Clara County, California and the federal courts located in the Northern District of the State of California shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The parties consent to the exercise by such courts of personal jurisdiction over them and each party waives any objection it might otherwise have to venue, personal jurisdiction, inconvenience of forum, and any similar or related doctrine.', it allows a party to dispute the jurisdiction of the specified courts. This directly contradicts the previous statement that the courts 'shall have exclusive jurisdiction', as a party could now potentially challenge jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law princip les. The parties agree that the state courts of Santa Clara County, California and the federal courts located in the Northern District of the State of California shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit waiver of objections to jurisdiction, which directly contradicts the statement in both the original and contradicted texts that the specified courts 'shall have exclusive jurisdiction'. By removing the waiver, a party could now potentially challenge the court's jurisdiction, creating uncertainty and contradicting the original intention of exclusive jurisdiction. The omission introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Information That Is Not Confidential. Information in the foll owing categories shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement: (a) information which is in the public domain at the time of the receipt under this Agreement; (b) information which comes into the public domain after receipt under th is Agreement without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (c) information which the Recipient can show was in the Recipient’s possession before the date of disclosure under this Agreement; (d) information that the Recipient can show was acquired by the Recipient from a third party who was not known by the Recipient to be under an obligation of confidence to Sanmina; and (e) information which the Recipient can show was independently developed by the Recipient without reference to Sanmina’s Confidenti al Information .
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Information That Is Not Confidential. Information in the foll owing categories shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement: (a) information which is in the public domain at the time of the receipt under this Agreement; (b) information which comes into the public domain after receipt under th is Agreement without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the remaining categories that define what is not considered Confidential Information (c, d and e), the agreement lacks clear guidance on the exceptions. This directly contradicts the introductory sentence of this section which states that 'Information *in the following categories* shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement', because now there are only 2 categories, making the prior language untrue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Information That Is Not Confidential. Information in the foll owing categories shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement: (a) information which is in the public domain at the time of the receipt under this Agreement; (b) information which comes into the public domain after receipt under th is Agreement without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient;
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes categories (c, d, and e) of information that are not considered confidential. This omission directly contradicts the introductory sentence of Section 3, which explicitly states that 'Information in the following categories shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement.' By reducing the number of listed categories, the amended text renders the original statement false, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text and the changed text are in the same location within the document, further emphasizing the direct conflict.
